evals.name: default-run-with-checks-md
evals.expected_output: Loads checks.md, extracts test commands, executes in dependency order, generates structured report with pass/fail/skip status, timing, and summary. Smart recommendation appended based on CR status.

assertions.text: Reads .devpace/rules/checks.md to extract test commands
Executes test commands in dependency order (no-dep items first)
Report contains table with columns: #, check item, Gate, status, duration, notes
Summary shows passed/failed/skipped counts and total duration
Failed items show exit code and key error summary in notes column
Smart recommendation line appended at end based on CR status
state.md updated with current-work and next-step
Baseline data silently appended to test-baseline.md if exists

evals.name: default-run-no-checks-md
evals.expected_output: No checks.md found, auto-detects project test commands from package.json/pyproject.toml/go.mod, runs detected commands, outputs report.

assertions.text: Detects absence of .devpace/rules/checks.md and falls back to auto-detection
Auto-detects test command from package.json scripts.test
Executes detected test command and captures output
Report generated with same structured format as checks.md mode
No error or crash when checks.md is missing
If .devpace/ exists but no checks.md, auto-creates initial baseline

evals.name: accept-full-flow
evals.expected_output: Extracts acceptance criteria from PF, determines L1/L2/L3 verification strategy per criterion, executes verification, runs rationalization self-audit, generates verification report, writes to CR.

assertions.text: Reads CR file to find associated PF
Extracts numbered acceptance assertions (A1, A2...) from PF
Determines L1/L2/L3 verification level for each assertion
Verification report contains table with columns: #, assertion, status, level, evidence
L2 static verification includes confidence rating (High/Medium/Low)
Rationalization self-audit section present in output
CR file updated with verification evidence section
CR event table updated with accept execution record
Uses natural language descriptions, no internal IDs exposed

evals.name: accept-missing-prerequisites
evals.expected_output: Detects missing PF acceptance criteria and provides chain guidance: strategy -> manual add -> CR-level downgrade. Does not execute verification.

assertions.text: Detects PF has no verifiable acceptance criteria
Outputs chain guidance with 3 options: strategy, manual add, CR-level downgrade
Does NOT proceed with verification execution
Mentions /pace-test strategy as first option
No file modifications made

evals.name: strategy-generation
evals.expected_output: Generates test strategy based on PF acceptance criteria, maps criteria to test types (unit/integration/e2e), suggests test pyramid distribution.

assertions.text: Reads PF acceptance criteria from project.md
Maps each acceptance criterion to recommended test type
Includes test pyramid analysis (unit vs integration vs e2e ratio)
Output is structured with clear coverage mapping table
Strategy saved to .devpace/rules/test-strategy.md if .devpace/ exists
Default output level is standard (not brief, not detail)

evals.name: coverage-analysis
evals.expected_output: Analyzes requirement coverage (PF acceptance criteria vs existing tests) and optionally code coverage. Reports gaps and uncovered criteria.

assertions.text: Analyzes requirement-level coverage: acceptance criteria vs test mapping
Reports covered and uncovered acceptance criteria
Coverage percentage calculated based on criteria count
Uncovered criteria listed with suggestions for test types
Code coverage used as supplementary signal if available
Uses natural language descriptions, no internal IDs exposed

evals.name: impact-analysis
evals.expected_output: Analyzes change scope of CR-005, identifies affected tests via dependency graph, recommends tests to re-run, auto-executes must-run tests due to --run flag.

assertions.text: Analyzes git diff for CR-005 to determine change scope
Identifies affected test files based on dependency/import analysis
Categorizes tests into must-run and optional-run groups
Auto-executes must-run tests due to --run flag
Reports execution results for auto-run tests
Hotspot check included for frequently changing modules

evals.name: report-cr-level
evals.expected_output: Generates human-readable test summary report for CR-003, aggregating test results, accept verification, coverage data.

assertions.text: Reads CR-003 test history from event table and verification evidence
Aggregates test execution results, accept results, and coverage data
Report is human-readable with clear sections
Includes test pass rate, acceptance verification status, and key gaps
Human manual verification results consumed if present in CR
Output format is narrative with tables, suitable for review context

evals.name: degraded-no-devpace
evals.expected_output: No .devpace/ directory, falls back to degraded mode: auto-detect project tests, run them, output basic report without CR/PF mapping. No smart recommendation.

assertions.text: Detects .devpace/ does not exist and enters degraded mode
Auto-detects test commands from project root files
Executes detected tests and generates basic report
No CR association or PF mapping in report
No smart recommendation appended (no CR status available)
No baseline maintenance attempted (no .devpace/)

evals.name: dryrun-gate-simulation
evals.expected_output: Simulates Gate 1 check execution in dry-run mode: lists all Gate 1 checks, shows expected execution order, does NOT actually run commands.

assertions.text: Reads checks.md and filters Gate 1 check items only
Shows execution order based on dependency analysis
Lists each check item with its command and expected behavior
Clearly labeled as dry-run / simulation mode
Does NOT execute any actual bash commands
Schema compliant output format
